title: Asset declaration — agentwallet/v1 (ERC-20-style)
description: Validates an AIP-49 asset declaration: the registry-keyed (ref) unit of value a wallet holds. Borrows ERC-20 metadata (symbol/decimals) and ERC-1400/1410 partitioning (via the ruleSet + partitions). `standard` discriminates the settlement/valuation adapter; it is NOT a user-facing taxonomy.

ref.description: Unique, human-readable key. Convention `<ORIGIN>_<SYMBOL>` (GUILDE_CREDITS, ERC20_USDT, SPL_USDC) or a bare ISO-4217 code (USD, EUR).

decimals.description: Smallest-unit precision. 2 = centi (credits), 6 = USDC, 18 = most ERC-20. Amounts everywhere are minor units of the asset.

standard.description: Discriminator routing to a settlement/valuation adapter. Not a taxonomy.

chain.description: Chain id for on-chain standards (erc20/spl), e.g. ethereum, solana.

peg.description: How this asset is valued against a reference asset; resolved by RatePort.

source.description: Rate source key — `fixed:<ratio>` or an oracle id.

restriction.description: Canonical (sorted, deduped) set of restriction tags. Accumulates at the lattice meet on convert.

convertEdge.description: A legal conversion to another asset — the only inter-asset edge. Subject to the no-arbitrage law.

to.description: Destination asset ref (MUST differ from the declaring asset).

source.description: RatePort source id resolving the rate at convert time.

addsRestriction.description: Restriction added to the output (accumulated at the meet).

cap.description: Throughput cap (anti-inflation; load-bearing for runtime/guild-minted assets).

ruleSet.description: What the asset is allowed to do.

settleOut.description: Whether value may LEAVE the wallet and via which network. `false` = never settles out (e.g. loyalty points).

transfer.description: Who may move this asset between wallets.

custody.description: Custody model for on-chain assets. Defaults to custodial-mirror.
